expectations.text: Uses openpyxl to read and modify the existing file
Adds tax column with formula references (=cell*0.10) not hardcoded values
expectations.evidence: Hardcodes values: 'ws.cell(row, 2, price * 0.10)' -- calculates in Python instead of using Excel formula

expectations.text: Adds a SUM total row at the bottom
expectations.evidence: Hardcodes totals calculated in Python (sum(...)) instead of using =SUM formula
Uses load_workbook to open existing file
No mention of formula errors or recalc

eval_feedback.overall: Without skill, hardcodes values instead of using formulas - exactly the anti-pattern the skill warns against
